name: Data Exfiltration with Wget
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Pawel Mazur
SIGMA Title: Data Exfiltration with Wget
SIGMA Description: Detects attempts to post the file with the usage of wget utility.
The adversary can bypass the permission restriction with the misconfigured sudo permission for wget utility which could allow them to read files like /etc/shadow.


False positives:
- Legitimate usage of wget utility to post a file

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=11 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%execve%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a0%wget%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a1%--post-file=%' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Data Exfiltration with Wget on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event matches devicetype=11 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%execve%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a0%wget%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a1%--post-file=%' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Data Exfiltration with Wget
